join_order.notes: Use the dense sub adsh lookup first so only pre rows whose adsh survives fy=2023 contribute downstream.

join_order.build: pre(stmt='IS' and sub.fy=2023) keyed by (adsh,tag,version) -> plabel[]
join_order.probe: num via indexes/adsh_tag_version_sorted with row-level uom='USD' check
join_order.notes: Probe the existing num sorted triple index instead of scanning num; keep only matching runs and aggregate matching num rows.

data_structures.fact_join: compact_hash_map(pre_sized) keyed by filtered (adsh,tag,version) with contiguous plabel payloads; probe num pre_built_index
data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map sized to observed join cardinality, allocated lazily per worker and merged once

optimization_notes: Root cause: the current plan does unnecessary work in the wrong order and uses cache-exceeding runtime structures, specifically a filtered-pre hash table larger than LLC and oversized thread-local aggregation maps while still scanning nearly all num rows. Strategy category A with work elimination: filter sub first, scan pre sequentially with stmt zone-map pruning and the sub adsh filter to build only the surviving triple->plabel payloads, then probe num through the existing sorted (adsh,tag,version) index instead of full-scanning num; aggregate with right-sized lazy worker maps so the working set stays closer to cache and the 904 ms main scan disappears.
